mission.title: Install and Configure K3s (Lightweight Kubernetes)
mission.description: K3s is a lightweight Kubernetes distribution designed for resource-constrained environments such as edge computing and IoT. It simplifies the installation and management of Kubernetes by packaging it as a single binary, making it ideal for users who need a production-ready solution without the complexity of traditional Kubernetes setups. Key trade-offs include reduced features compared to full Kubernetes and reliance on a single binary for operation.

steps.title: Install the K3s binary
steps.description:
This step installs the K3s binary on your server, which is necessary to run the K3s Kubernetes distribution.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

```

> This command downloads and installs the latest version of K3s.

steps.title: Verify K3s installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the K3s service to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

systemctl status k3s

```

> This command shows the current status of the K3s service.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure the kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to communicate with your K3s cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

chmod 600 /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

```

> This command sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable to the K3s kubeconfig file.
> This command ensures the kubeconfig file has the correct permissions.

steps.title: Verify nodes in the cluster
steps.description:
Check that the nodes are correctly registered in the K3s cluster.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the K3s cluster.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
Set up additional features such as metrics server and monitoring.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases/latest/download/components.yaml

```

> This command installs the metrics server for resource monitoring.

uninstall.title: Uninstall K3s
uninstall.description:
This step removes K3s and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all workloads and data in the cluster.

```bash

/usr/local/bin/k3s-uninstall.sh

rm -rf /etc/rancher/k3s

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade K3s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster data. The upgrade process will replace the existing K3s binary with the new version.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > k3s-backup.yaml

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources in the cluster.
> This command upgrades K3s to the latest version.
> Verify that nodes are still registered after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k3s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The K3s service may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'journalctl -u k3s' and restart the service with 'systemctl restart k3s'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not be correctly set up or permissions may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure KUBECONFIG is set correctly and permissions are set with 'chmod 600 /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The container image may not be available or the node may not have internet access.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the node has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have any workloads deployed.

**Fix:**
Deploy a sample application using 'kubectl apply -f <sample-app.yaml>'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new CNI plugins, improved metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for certain legacy storage backends.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to update your storage classes accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your environment meets the minimum resource requirements.
